2603.17592.title: A Contextual Help Browser Extension to Assist Digital Illiterate Internet Users
2603.17592.paper_summary: This paper describes the design, implementation, and evaluation of a browser extension that provides contextual help to users who hover over technological acronyms and abbreviations on web pages. The extension combines a curated technical dictionary with OpenAI's large language model (LLM) to deliver on-demand definitions through lightweight tooltip overlays. A dual-layer artificial intelligence (AI) pipeline, comprising Google Cloud's Natural Language Processing (NLP) taxonomy API and OpenAI's ChatGPT, classifies each visited page as technology-related before activating the tooltip logic, thereby reducing false-positive detections. A mixed-methods study with 25 participants evaluated the tool's effect on reading comprehension and information-retrieval time among users with low to intermediate digital literacy. Results show that 92% of participants reported improved understanding of technical terms, 96% confirmed time savings over manual web searches, and all participants found the tooltips non-disruptive. Dictionary-based definitions were appended in an average of 2135 ms, compared to 16429 ms for AI-generated definitions and a mean manual search time of 17200 ms per acronym. The work demonstrates a practical, real-time approach to bridging the digital literacy gap and points toward extending contextual help to other domains such as medicine, law, and finance.
2603.17592.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文描述了一個瀏覽器擴展的設計、實施和評估，該擴展為在網頁上懸停於技術縮寫和簡稱的用戶提供上下文幫助。<lb/>該擴展將策劃的技術詞典與OpenAI的大型語言模型（LLM）結合，通過輕量級的工具提示覆蓋層提供隨需定義。<lb/>一個雙層人工智能（AI）管道，包括Google Cloud的自然語言處理（NLP）分類API和OpenAI的ChatGPT，在啟用工具提示邏輯之前，將每個訪問的頁面分類為技術相關，從而減少誤報檢測。<lb/>一項包含25名參與者的混合方法研究評估了該工具對於數字素養較低至中等的用戶在閱讀理解和信息檢索時間上的影響。<lb/>結果顯示，92%的參與者報告對技術術語的理解有所改善，96%的人確認比起手動網頁搜索節省了時間，所有參與者都認為工具提示不具干擾性。<lb/>基於詞典的定義平均附加時間為2135毫秒，而AI生成的定義為16429毫秒，每個縮寫的平均手動搜索時間為17200毫秒。<lb/>這項工作展示了一種實用的實時方法，以縮小數字素養差距，並指出可以將上下文幫助擴展到其他領域，如醫學、法律和金融。
2603.17592.author: Christos Koutsiaris et.al.
